macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Stock Futures Decline on Iran Conflict Concerns
items.summary: US stock futures fell amid escalating tensions in Iran after a negative response to a US peace plan and Gulf states' condemnation of attacks on energy infrastructure. Oil prices surged with Brent crude up 3.9% to $106.2 and WTI up 3.61% to $93.58 per barrel.

items.title: Major Indices Close Higher Despite Middle East Risks
items.summary: On March 26, the Dow rose 0.7% to 46,429.49, Nasdaq gained 0.8% to 21,929.83, and S&P 500 increased 0.5% to 6,591.90, led by sectors like Materials, Health Care, and Consumer Discretionary. Super Micro Computer shares jumped 8.2% as the top S&P 500 gainer.

items.title: Intraday Pullback in Major Indices After ECB Comments
items.summary: Midday on March 26, Nasdaq dropped 1.57%, Russell 2000 fell 1.24%, and S&P 500 declined 1.15% following ECB remarks, reversing prior gains fueled by Iran deal hopes. Markets are adjusting risk premiums amid Middle East tensions.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $70,000 Amid Weakening Short-Term Momentum
items.summary: Bitcoin has dropped below $70,000 as buyers become more selective, entering a phase of cautious reassessment rather than capitulation. The market retains growth potential but favors stronger assets amid ongoing ETF developments.

items.title: US Regulators Issue New Clarifications on Crypto Asset Status
items.summary: American regulators have provided new clarifications distinguishing crypto assets like digital goods, stablecoins, and investment contracts, reducing uncertainty and aiding institutional strategies. This shift supports tokenization of real assets and lowers risk discounts for Bitcoin and major tokens.

items.title: $13.2 Billion Bitcoin Options Expiry Triggers Major Volatility Event
items.summary: A massive $13.2 billion Bitcoin options expiry on Deribit, with a $75,000 max pain price and 0.59 put/call ratio, marks one of the largest single-day events in crypto history. It highlights market sophistication alongside a $2.1 billion Ethereum expiry.

items.title: SEC Advances New Regulations for Cryptocurrency
items.summary: The SEC is moving forward with new regulations for crypto, as reported from the Digital Asset Summit, impacting consumers and the industry. Details aim to provide clearer frameworks for digital assets.

items.title: FBI Reports Soaring Crypto Crime Including Scams
items.summary: Crypto-related crimes, such as investment and romance scams, are surging according to FBI data, with funds moving rapidly and complicating recovery efforts.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Washington Governor Signs Landmark AI Safety Bills
items.summary: Gov. Bob Ferguson signed two AI-related bills into law, including SB 1786 requiring provenance data for generative AI media and HB 2592 mandating state agencies to adopt AI to reduce burdens.

items.title: Meta Releases TRIBE v2 Brain Response Prediction Model
items.summary: Meta introduced TRIBE v2, a foundation model trained on 500+ hours of fMRI data from 700+ people to predict human brain responses to sights and sounds, enabling zero-shot predictions across subjects and tasks.

items.title: Cursor Unveils Fast Regex Search for AI Agent Tools
items.summary: Cursor developed a local sparse n-gram index to replace ripgrep, reducing grep latency from 15+ seconds in large monorepos by pre-filtering regex matches; code and checkpoints are publicly available.

items.title: Salesforce Announces AI Foundry for Agentic AI Trends
items.summary: Salesforce AI Research highlighted simulation environments like eVerse, agent-to-agent ecosystems, and ambient intelligence as key trends through 2027, launching AI Foundry to enable enterprise general intelligence.

items.title: Google Launches Gemini 3.1 Flash Live and Search Live Globally
items.summary: Google released Gemini 3.1 Flash Live for more natural audio AI and expanded Search Live globally, alongside developer tools for financial assistants with LlamaParse and Gemini, and Google I/O 2026 save-the-date AI experience.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Sony Closes Dark Outlaw Games
items.summary: Sony has shut down Dark Outlaw Games, as discussed in recent gaming headlines alongside Marathon's PC popularity and the upcoming Xbox Partner Preview.

items.title: Xbox Partner Preview March 2026 Reveals Major Game Updates
items.summary: Xbox's first third-party showcase of 2026 featured gameplay for The Expanse: Osiris Reborn, Alien Deathstorm, S.T.A.L.K.E.R. 2 Coast of Hope DLC, Artificial Detective, and more.

items.title: Gameloft Brisbane Announces Bluey: Happy Snaps
items.summary: A new Bluey video game titled Happy Snaps, a photography simulation encouraging exploration, is in development at Gameloft's Brisbane studio.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Borderlands 4 March Patch Improves PC Performance
items.summary: Borderlands 4 received a March patch that significantly boosts PC performance, with native FPS improvements noted in the update. This addresses key player feedback on optimization.

items.title: Asian Memory Chip Stocks Stumble on Google Memory Breakthrough
items.summary: Asian memory chip stocks declined amid reports of a Google memory breakthrough, contributing to market pressures alongside geopolitical tensions. This development raises concerns for the sector's competitiveness.

items.title: Trump Appoints Tech Leaders to Presidential Technology Council
items.summary: President Trump appointed Mark Zuckerberg, Marc Andreessen, and Jensen Huang to a new presidential technology council focusing on science and technology policy. David Sacks discussed the 'star power' lineup.

items.title: Apple Announces WWDC for June 8
items.summary: Apple's Annual Worldwide Developers Conference is scheduled for June 8, expected to showcase upcoming software and hardware updates. This event highlights Apple's developer ecosystem plans.

items.title: Roseman University Launches 120,000 sq ft Life Science Incubator
items.summary: Roseman University has launched Roseman Bioventures, a major 120,000-square-foot life science incubator to foster biotech innovation. This facility aims to support emerging life sciences ventures.
